bead.title: agent: update Step 0 decomposition instructions and prompts
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Agent-facing Step 0 guidance still tells workers not to close the current bead and only permits parent-note updates during decomposition. That prevents agents from applying the corrected lifecycle rule after a lossless split and leaves generated child readiness semantics ambiguous.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:210-213 and .claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md:210-213 forbid `ddx bead close <bead-id>` for the current bead and only allow a parent-note update during Step 0 decomposition.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go and cli/internal/agent/testdata/execute_bead_prompt_snapshots/{agent,claude}.txt derive or snapshot the same instruction shape, so prompt tests will continue to encode the old behavior unless updated.

PROPOSED FIX
Update .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md, .claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md, cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go, and the agent/claude prompt snapshots so Step 0 says agents may create child, sibling, or replacement beads for a lossless split; may close the decomposed parent after the split is durable; must wire only legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement dependencies; and must not add the decomposed parent as a child dependency. Keep generated adapter conventions intact and update snapshots through the repo's normal prompt-generation path where available.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the broader `parent_ancestor_in_deps` doctor/back-edge repair slice tracked by ddx-130cf9ab. This bead must not regress it and should reuse its invariant, but it does not replace that repair/backfill work.
- Do not redesign epic auto-close or historical `dead_intermediate_close`; keep those as legacy/backfill behavior for already-open containers.
- Do not drain or close existing decomposed queue entries as part of this bead. File or run a separate reconciliation bead if historical tracker cleanup is needed.
- Do not change Fizeau routing, model escalation, or provider selection.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b.
Related, not dependency: ddx-130cf9ab.
Governing artifacts: FEAT-010 and TD-031 must remain consistent with the instruction wording updated by the documentation child bead.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b.
Deps: No deps. This child can land before or after the docs child but must not contradict the final governed contract.
bead.acceptance:
1. .agents/skills/ddx/reference/work.md and .claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md instruct Step 0 decomposition agents that a lossless split may close the decomposed parent after child/sibling/replacement beads are durably filed.
2. The same Step 0 guidance says generated children may carry `Parent=<decomposed-id>` but must not include the decomposed parent in dependency IDs; only child-to-child or sibling/replacement deps are allowed when independently justified.
3. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go emits the corrected Step 0 decomposition instructions.
4. cli/internal/agent/testdata/execute_bead_prompt_snapshots/agent.txt and cli/internal/agent/testdata/execute_bead_prompt_snapshots/claude.txt match the corrected prompt output.
5. Add or update `TestExecuteBeadPrompt_Step0DecompositionMayCloseParent` and `TestExecuteBeadPrompt_DecompositionForbidsParentDependency` or equivalent prompt coverage.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestExecuteBeadPrompt_Step0DecompositionMayCloseParent|TestExecuteBeadPrompt_DecompositionForbidsParentDependency|DecomposeDoesNotSelfDep' -count=1` passes.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
